step_by_step_thinking:
Micro porosity refers specifically to the formation of tiny void spaces during the initial freezing of metallic alloys within molds. This phenomenon arises simultaneously from the nucleation of solid grains and gas bubbles as the alloy begins to freeze against the mold wall. Given the context, option **C** directly corresponds to this description.
\boxed{C} Based on the information presented, the term being described matches the definition of micro porosity perfectly—it involves minute cavities forming immediately upon the start of freezing involving both solidification nuclei and entrapped gases.
